pattern: Implementing a set of integers from scratch with bucket arrays shows how a hash function plus per-bucket membership lists turns add, remove, and lookup into near-constant-time operations.
hints: Scatter each key into one of a fixed number of buckets using the remainder of the key by the bucket count.
A bucket is just a short list of the keys that landed there, since distinct keys can collide into the same bucket.
To add, append the key to its bucket only if absent; to remove, drop it from the bucket; to test membership, scan the bucket for the key.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: add, query present and absent, re-add duplicate, remove, query removed
contains on an empty set is false
adding the same key twice does not create a duplicate; one remove clears it
removing a key that was never added is harmless
remove then re-add the same key restores membership
key zero is a real, storable value

stress.description: long randomized add/remove/contains workload over a small key space so collisions, duplicate adds, and misses all occur
stress.note: small key range forces hash collisions handled by chaining
